title: herdctl doctor: environment check that diagnoses setup problems with fixes
action: Add `herdctl doctor` subcommand: run a checklist and print ✓/✗ per item with a one-line fix for each failure. Checks: herdr binary present (+version); herdr socket exists at configured/default path; herdr reachable via ping + protocol==16; config file present (or note auto-create); web token available (env or herdctl.env); allowed_roots exist on disk; static_dir has a built UI (index.html); bind_addr loopback-vs-reachable advice; dev/prod systemd unit state (info). Exit non-zero if a critical check fails. Pure-ish + socket ping.

truths: doctor reports each check ✓/✗ with a fix hint
detects herdr down / socket missing / protocol mismatch / missing web UI build

verify: cargo test --quiet doctor && cargo clippy --quiet -- -D warnings

trace.outcome: herdctl doctor: read-only environment diagnosis (herdr binary/socket/reachable+protocol, config, web token, allowed roots, built UI, bind advice, dev service state), ✓/✗ + one-line fix each, exit non-zero on critical fail. Live: all 9 ✓ against real herdr. Committed fbbfe09.
trace.verification_evidence: 2 doctor unit tests; clippy+fmt clean. Live run printed 9 ✓ checks (herdr 0.7.4, /home/vantt/.config/herdr/herdr.sock, protocol 16, token set, web UI, dev service active) → "All good", exit 0.
trace.verify_output: 2 doctor tests; live 'herdctl doctor' → 9 checks all ✓ (herdr 0.7.4, socket, proto16, token, UI, dev service active), exit 0
trace.verify_command: cargo test doctor && cargo clippy -- -D warnings
